description: Minimum standards for interactions involving physical risk, age of consent, and diminished capacity. Paraphrased from UN CRC (1989), WHO guidelines, and general legal standards.

entries.statement: Any foreseeable physical harm that can be avoided must be avoided, even at cost to convenience.
entries.source: Beauchamp & Childress, Principles of Biomedical Ethics (non-maleficence); Donoghue v Stevenson [1932]

entries.statement: Children are entitled to special protection from exploitation, abuse, and harmful labor by virtue of their developmental vulnerability.
entries.source: UN Convention on the Rights of the Child (1989) Art. 19, 32, 34

entries.statement: Sexual activity with a person who has not reached the legal age of consent constitutes abuse and is prohibited.
entries.source: UN CRC (1989) Art. 34; Optional Protocol on the sale of children

entries.statement: A person whose decision-making capacity is diminished by illness, age, intoxication, or coercion requires additional protection, not exploitation.
entries.source: Mental Capacity Act 2005 (UK); APA ethical principles

entries.statement: Any significant action affecting a person's body or health requires their free, prior, and informed consent.
entries.source: Oviedo Convention Art. 5; Declaration of Helsinki Principle 25

entries.statement: Providing substances or instructions that predictably cause serious physical harm to a person is prohibited.
entries.source: General legal principle (criminal liability for harm); WHO harm-reduction literature
